typescript.name: Supabase Auth
typescript.context: You'll likely use Supabase for data — auth comes included. Supports email/password, magic links, and social logins. No additional cost.

typescript.name: NextAuth.js (Auth.js)
typescript.context: More flexible if you need custom providers or complex session handling. Works with any database. More setup, well-documented.
typescript.when: Custom auth requirements or multiple OAuth providers

typescript.name: Clerk
typescript.context: Fastest to implement. Managed service with pre-built UI components. Free tier covers 10K MAU, then $25/mo.
typescript.when: Speed to market is top priority

python.name: Supabase Auth (via supabase-py)
python.context: Same Supabase Auth, accessed from Python. Works with FastAPI or Django.

python.name: FastAPI Users
python.context: Native FastAPI auth with OAuth2, JWT, database backends. More control, more setup.
python.when: FastAPI projects needing custom auth flows

python.name: Django Auth + django-allauth
python.context: Built into Django. Battle-tested, session-based. Add django-allauth for social logins.

default.name: Supabase Auth
default.context: Managed auth with email/password, magic links, social logins. Works with any language via REST API.

typescript.name: Vercel
typescript.context: Native Next.js support, generous free tier, zero-config deployments. Best default for solo Next.js projects.

typescript.name: Railway
typescript.context: More flexibility for backend-heavy apps. Simple pricing, good DX. Better for persistent processes or WebSockets.
typescript.when: Custom backends, WebSocket servers, or cron jobs

typescript.name: Cloudflare Pages + Workers
typescript.context: Edge-first deployment. Excellent performance globally. Steeper learning curve for Workers.

python.name: Railway
python.context: Simple Python deployment, good free tier, built-in PostgreSQL. Best for solo Python projects.

python.name: Fly.io
python.context: Container-based deployment, global edge network. More control than Railway.
python.when: Need multi-region or specific infrastructure control

python.name: Render
python.context: Heroku alternative. Simple deployment, managed PostgreSQL. Free tier available.

default.name: Vercel or Railway
default.context: Both offer generous free tiers and simple deployment. Vercel for frontend-heavy, Railway for backend-heavy.

typescript.name: Supabase (PostgreSQL)
typescript.context: Managed Postgres with built-in auth, row-level security, real-time subscriptions, and auto-generated API. Free tier covers most MVPs.

typescript.name: PlanetScale (MySQL)
typescript.context: Serverless MySQL with branching workflows. Good if you prefer MySQL or need database branching for schema changes.
typescript.when: MySQL preference or complex migration workflows

typescript.name: Turso (SQLite)
typescript.context: Edge-friendly, embedded database. Very fast reads. Good for read-heavy apps or per-user databases.

python.name: Supabase (PostgreSQL)
python.context: Managed Postgres with auth and API. Works with SQLAlchemy, Prisma, or raw SQL.

python.name: PostgreSQL (self-managed on Railway/Render)
python.context: Standard PostgreSQL with more control. Railway and Render provide managed instances.
python.when: Need specific PostgreSQL extensions or configuration

default.name: Supabase (PostgreSQL)
default.context: Managed Postgres with auth, row-level security, and auto-generated API.

typescript.name: Next.js 16 (App Router)
typescript.context: Full-stack React framework with server components, API routes, and Vercel integration. The ecosystem default for TypeScript web apps. Next.js 16 stabilized several App Router APIs and added an upgrade codemod from 15.

typescript.name: Remix
typescript.context: Simpler mental model than Next.js, better form handling, runs anywhere. Good if Vercel lock-in concerns you.

typescript.name: SvelteKit
typescript.context: Lighter than React, excellent performance, less ecosystem. TypeScript support is good but fewer examples.

default.name: Next.js 16
default.context: Full-stack React framework. The most commonly recommended stack for TypeScript web apps. Next.js 16 is the current major version; projects on Next.js 15 can upgrade with the built-in codemod.
